commit.name: Weather Card
commit.description: Data display with props, design tokens, and responsive layout
commit.prompt:
Build a weather card component. It should display:
- Current temperature (large, prominent)
- Weather condition with an appropriate icon/emoji (sunny, cloudy, rainy, etc.)
- Humidity percentage
- Wind speed
- A 5-day forecast strip at the bottom showing day name, icon, and high/low temps

All data must come from props — never hardcode weather data. Use CSS variables from the design system for all colors and spacing.

issues.description: No hover/focus states on forecast items. They appear static and don't provide visual feedback on interaction.
issues.fix: Add CSS transitions and hover effects to forecast Box components (scale, background color change on hover)

issues.description: Weather emoji/icons are not accessible. Screen readers will read the raw emoji without context, and there's no aria-label describing weather conditions.
issues.fix: Add aria-label to weather icon elements (e.g., aria-label='Sunny weather') and consider adding alt text or title attributes to forecast icons

issues.description: Humidity and Wind Speed boxes lack semantic structure and ARIA labels for the values they contain.
issues.fix: Add aria-label to Box elements containing metrics (e.g., aria-label='Humidity: 65%') and consider wrapping in <article> or semantic container

issues.description: Forecast items use a light blue background that may not have sufficient contrast with some text colors in all themes.
issues.fix: Ensure contrast ratio meets WCAG AA standards (4.5:1 for normal text) by testing with color checker or adjusting to use border instead of background

issues.description: Wind speed unit is hardcoded as 'm/s' but props don't include a windSpeedUnit parameter, limiting flexibility.
issues.fix: Add optional windSpeedUnit prop with default value 'm/s' to allow customization (e.g., 'mph', 'km/h')

evaluations.critique: The component successfully displays all required weather data with good visual hierarchy and proper use of design tokens. The layout is clean and responsive, with the prominent temperature display and well-organized forecast strip. However, the component lacks interactive polish (no hover states on forecast items) and has accessibility gaps—emoji icons need aria-labels, and the metric boxes should be more semantically structured for screen readers. Adding smooth transitions and improving ARIA labeling would elevate this to production-ready quality.

evaluation.critique: The component has solid data handling and responsive layout structure, but has some design token inconsistencies with hardcoded fallback values (hex colors) and inline style usage instead of proper spacing variables. Polish is adequate but lacks interactive states and transitions that would enhance the weather card experience.
